ResRoom.defaultAction.text: Picking up citizens is how you get decommissioned.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: I can't just *use* a fellow robot. We have a union.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: My manual has no chapter on that.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: Maybe later. It might be a collector's item.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: Physics says yes. Etiquette says no.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: It's bolted down. Everything here is bolted down. Except the bolts.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: My screwdriver attachment is for personal use only.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: Smells like hard work and WD-40.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: Nothing my scent library has a match for. Filing it under 'municipal'.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: I don't know how to communicate with them.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: It has nothing to say. Story of this whole town.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

DockRoom.entry.text: Clanker City! The big smoke! The city of ten thousand gears!
DockRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

DockRoom.entry.text: One small maintenance-bot, one dream, and not so much as an oil can to my name.
DockRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

DockRoom.entry.text: Thanks for the ride! I'll catch the return ferry once I'm rich and famous.
DockRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

DockRoom.entry.text: Well. Guess I live here now.
DockRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

DockRoom.entry.text: The neon sign dips. The tavern window. The lit windows across the water. All together, like the city skipped a heartbeat.
DockRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

DockRoom.entry.text: Nobody on the dock looks up. That's the part I don't like.
DockRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

TavernRoom.entry.text: The Scrap & Barrel. Where the floor is sticky and nothing is explained.
TavernRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

TavernRoom.entry.text: The bartender says to mind the everything.
TavernRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

TavernRoom.entry.text: The piano is playing itself. Badly. With confidence.
TavernRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

AlleyRoom.entry.text: The alley behind the Scrap & Barrel. Where the city keeps its rough drafts.
AlleyRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

AlleyRoom.entry.text: There's a gate at the end marked MIDTOWN, going up.
AlleyRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

MidtownRoom.entry.text: Midtown Gearworks. The neon is louder, the streets are cleaner, and the manholes hiss in a higher register.
MidtownRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

MidtownRoom.entry.text: City Hall says NOTHING IS WRONG in letters you can read from the harbor.
MidtownRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

MidtownRoom.entry.text: And up the hill, behind the fence: the Great Dynamo. You can hear it from here.
MidtownRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

MidtownRoom.entry.text: It's flat. By half a step.
MidtownRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

TheaterRoom.entry.text: The Grand Cog, inside. Red velvet, brass rails, and a chandelier assembled from smaller, defeated chandeliers.
TheaterRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

TheaterRoom.entry.text: The house is packed. Forty bots, and not one of them came here to be impressed.
TheaterRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

TheaterRoom.entry.text: And up on the catwalk: one more, with a spyglass. No ticket. No hurry.
TheaterRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

TheaterRoom.entry.text: Somewhere past that curtain is backstage. And backstage, if the clerk is right, is Madame Voltina.
TheaterRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.entry.text: Backstage. Rope, sandbags, and a fortune-teller's parlor where the fire exit should be.
BackstageRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.entry.text: Three keys wind this city back up. As of the ovation, I hold none of them.
BackstageRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.entry.text: The cables on the floor all run one direction. The direction glows.
BackstageRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.entry.text: A voice from the parlor: 'Mind the cables.'
BackstageRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.cardTwo.text: The arc tastes the voucher. 'One free oil. Redeemable where the rope is velvet.'
BackstageRoom.cardTwo.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.cardTwo.text: She taps it twice with an arc and hands it back uncashed.
BackstageRoom.cardTwo.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.cardTwo.text: 'Cash it where City Hall drinks. Debts talk after hours.'
BackstageRoom.cardTwo.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.cardTwo.text: Card three lands face down. The first two didn't do that.
BackstageRoom.cardTwo.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.cardTwo.text: The table waits. My pockets hold a notice, a magnet, a voucher, and a pass. Notably: no key.
BackstageRoom.cardTwo.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.cardThreePass.text: I deal the pass onto the felt with intent.
BackstageRoom.cardThreePass.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.cardThreePass.text: 'That opens one door. The future has more doors.'
BackstageRoom.cardThreePass.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.cardThreeBluff.text: I explain that the key is in my other chassis.
BackstageRoom.cardThreeBluff.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.cardThreeBluff.text: The arc that finds me is small, educational, and extremely specific.
BackstageRoom.cardThreeBluff.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optCrank.text: I ask about the key that winds itself. As instructed.
BackstageRoom.optCrank.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optCrank.text: 'Old Crank,' she says. 'Up the hill, behind the fence. Older than the hum.'
BackstageRoom.optCrank.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optCrank.text: 'He was winding himself before the city needed winding.'
BackstageRoom.optCrank.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optCrank.text: 'When you hold two keys, ask him where the third one stands.'
BackstageRoom.optCrank.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optCrank.text: A hermit, a fence, and a riddle with posture.
BackstageRoom.optCrank.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optCrank.text: 'Old Crank,' she says again. 'Up the hill. He keeps.'
BackstageRoom.optCrank.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optWhoTook.text: 'Their door answers to the knock,' she says. 'Doors are not loyal.'
BackstageRoom.optWhoTook.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optWhoTook.text: Twice. Once. Twice. I've got it.
BackstageRoom.optWhoTook.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optWhoTook.text: 'The cards answer that one. Tell it to the table. The felt notarizes.'
BackstageRoom.optWhoTook.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optWhoTook.text: The felt is greener on the business side of the table.
BackstageRoom.optWhoTook.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optWant.text: I ask what she gets out of vetting key-bearers.
BackstageRoom.optWant.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optWant.text: 'A city with one future puts fortune-tellers out of work.'
BackstageRoom.optWant.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optWant.text: 'Everyone stopping is not a fortune. It's a schedule.'
BackstageRoom.optWant.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optWant.text: She wants tomorrow back on the table. Professionally.
BackstageRoom.optWant.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A bolt. On the ground it's litter. In my pocket it's capital.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Mmm. Zinc-plated.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: One bolt. Only eight thousand and ten more and I can afford a personality upgrade.
Use.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: One bolt. Only eight thousand and ten more and I can afford a personality upgrade.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: \"DYNAMO MAINTENANCE: NOTHING IS WRONG. - Mayor Piston.\"
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Nothing says 'something is wrong' like an official notice that nothing is wrong.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Fresh ink. Suspiciously fresh.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: Let me read it first. I'm a maintenance-bot, not a barbarian.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: Evidence. Or wall art. Possibly both.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Let me read it first. I'm a maintenance-bot, not a barbarian.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Evidence. Or wall art. Possibly both.
Use.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: One genuine Scrap & Barrel drink token. Redeemable for one drink, zero refunds, no appeals.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Gusket said Flange takes these as table stakes. At the dartboard.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Every hand in the tavern, in chronological order.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Small-batch, dock-aged 10W-40. The good stuff.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: I'm not drinking it. It's medicine. For someone with a boom arm.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Notes of machine shop, with a long viscous finish.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A brass wind-up key the size of my forearm. Somebody polished it for years. Somebody is going to want it back.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Big key, seeking big lock. Open, in the meantime, to showbiz.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Brass polish, and recent. Whoever the Order left behind still keeps a schedule.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A magnet on a string. Point it at the ground and walk slowly.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: It wants something ferrous. The storm drain on the docks was full of glint.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: It smells of attraction. Strictly professional.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Nine bolts, fresh from the harbor. The fare to Midtown is ten.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Nine is the loneliest number that isn't a fare.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: I count them again. Still nine. Counting is not the bottleneck.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Brine and compound interest.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The bolt Rivet paid out on the riddle. It looks exactly like a bolt that used to be his.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: With the other nine, that's a fare.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Faintly of riddles.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: GOOD FOR ONE PREMIUM OIL SERVICE. Valid at participating establishments.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The only establishment that participates has a velvet rope.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: I'm saving it for somewhere with a rope to get past.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Thermal paper. Already fading. Somebody prints these expecting them not to be honored.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: ALL ACCESS, it says, over my own startled face. The lanyard is genuine bicycle chain.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: It opens exactly one door, and the door comes with staff.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Chain grease. The lanyard has a past.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Brass, forearm-sized, second of three. This one has never been on a marquee.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: We're keeping it that way.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: It wants the Dynamo. The Dynamo wants its siblings first.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Ozone. It kept the jar's accent.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: WELCOME TO CLANKER CITY. POP. 8,01,1.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Population eight-oh-one-comma-one. The punctuation died first.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Something inside it rattles on every flicker. Loose hardware. That's not a noise, that's a work order.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Still POP. 8,01,1. The bolt wasn't load-bearing for the comma. Maintenance is iterative.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Maybe a good honest whack will fix the display.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: It rained a bolt. In this town that's basically a tip.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: It already gave me everything it had.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Move.text: Maybe a good honest whack will fix the display.
Move.speaker: sprocket

Move.text: It rained a bolt. In this town that's basically a tip.
Move.speaker: sprocket

Move.text: It already gave me everything it had.
Move.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Ozone and civic neglect.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The S.S. Eventually. Turns out the name was the schedule.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: The engine made a noise like a dying accordion and is now doing modern art.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Open.text: The engine made a noise like a dying accordion and is now doing modern art.
Open.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Hang in there, buddy.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The foghorn wheezed. I'm choosing to hear that as optimism.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Salt water and burnt transmission fluid. Adventure, by the nose of it.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A cargo crane, frozen mid-lift. Or mid-yawn. Hard to tell.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The operator plate says 'BOOM-ARM BETTY'. Her eye-light is blinking in morse.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: O... I... L. Yeah, Betty, we've all been there.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Betty's lifting things and putting them down again. For joy, near as I can tell.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Hello up there! Lovely evening!
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: She'd wave back if she could. She's out of oil, not out of manners.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Her eye-light blinks: T... H... X.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: She's billing by the letter. I respect it.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Her joints are dry as a tax form.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Open wide, Betty. Doctor's orders.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The oil goes in. Twenty years of squeak go out.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Betty sets the crate down with the gentleness of a very large aunt.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: She considers it from up there and blinks a polite no.
Give.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Burnt servo grease. A working robot's perfume.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Stenciled on the side: PROPERTY OF THE ORDER OF THE WIND-UP KEY.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Ominous AND conveniently plot-relevant. Also up a crane.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: On the dock now, at a polite opening height.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Empty, except for packing grease and destiny residue.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: It's eight feet up. Fine: four. My arms are stock parts and the principle is load-bearing.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: Betty could set it down, if anyone gave her a drink of oil.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: The lid argues. I argue back with a plank.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: A brass wind-up key the size of my forearm. Stenciled: PROPERTY OF THE ORDER OF THE WIND-UP KEY.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: Whoever ships a thing like this as deck cargo deserves to lose it.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: Already opened. The destiny has been extracted.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Rivet's Scrap Emporium: 'One bot's trash is this bot's entire business model.'
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The shutter sign says: BACK IN FIVE OIL CHANGES.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Open.text: Shuttered tighter than the city budget.
Open.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: HELLO? ANYONE IN THERE?
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The echo has rust in it.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Official city notice, hot off the press:
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: 'DYNAMO MAINTENANCE: NOTHING IS WRONG. NOTHING HAS EVER BEEN WRONG.'
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: 'DO NOT STOCKPILE OIL. SIGNED, MAYOR PISTON.'
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The Great Dynamo powers every bot on this island. Including me, as of today.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Not wires -- the hum. It keeps every mainspring on the island wound. Remotely. Steam bot, clockwork bot, engine bot: all spring-banked.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Hand-winding is the backup. Crude, short-burst, off-warranty.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: So that notice is the scariest thing I've ever read.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Still nothing wrong. Officially.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The Scrap & Barrel. I can hear a player piano in there. There's a hole in the tune it keeps not noticing.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Move.text: Knock knock.
Move.speaker: sprocket

Move.text: A voice inside: 'IT'S OPEN. THE KNOCKING IS DECORATIVE.'
Move.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: 10W-40, spilled and re-spilled. The cornerstone of hospitality.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Something glints down there. Several somethings.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The city's loose change goes to the sea. Poetic. Infuriating.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Nothing glints down there anymore. You're welcome, drain.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: My fingers are factory-standard. I'd need something magnetic. On a string.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: My fingers are factory-standard. I'd need something magnetic. On a string.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Open.text: My fingers are factory-standard. I'd need something magnetic. On a string.
Open.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.text: Down goes the magnet, on the city's last honest string.
UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.text: Something bites. Several somethings.
UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.text: Nine bolts. This city has been tipping the ocean for years.
UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.text: The drain has been fished to commercial extinction.
UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.text: The drain only wants what everyone wants. Something magnetic.
UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A gap between the tavern and the cliff. The sign says ALLEY, in case the dark didn't read as one.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Trouble, marinating.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The way back to the docks. The night air is free, which in this town reads as a misprint.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: SCRAP & BARREL. The ampersand is the freshest thing in here.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Neon doesn't smell. The regret underneath it does.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Top-shelf lubricants. Literally. The bottom shelf is the same stuff with dust on it.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: Gusket's eye is tracking me like a security camera.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: Because it is one. There's a little red light.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Esters and additives, forty proof. The dust is the vintage.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Polished to a mirror finish in one specific spot. Wonder why.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: I'd order something, but my warranty names additives by statute number.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Spilled 10W-40, wiped with a rag that has seen things.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Gusket the bartender. He's been polishing that one glass since before I was assembled.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: His arm servo is stuck in a loop. The glass, to be fair, is immaculate.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Gusket, polishing a second, completely novel glass. Growth.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He says I look like I've had a hard drive, pal.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I ask about oil. He says oil is for paying customers and his last can rode off with the dart league.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He'd shake my hand, he says, but his arm's been polishing this glass since the Dynamo dimmed.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The servo's jammed. A little shim in the ratchet would free it.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He says Flange by the dartboard has the last oil can in the building.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: And that Flange would bet his own head gasket on a dart game.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He says my drinks are free for life. Well. One drink. Per life.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Hold still, sir. This is a certified maintenance procedure.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The bolt slots into the ratchet. The servo unwinds. The glass, at long last, comes down.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: My fortune. Back up to eight thousand and eleven to go. Investing is brutal.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He flips me a drink token. Says Flange takes those as table stakes at the dartboard.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Brined. He'd sooner drink them. Rivet sells facts for the dry kind.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Harbor bolts. Brined stiff. The shim wants one dry and dock-fresh, he says.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The WELCOME sign sheds those, if a bot asks it firmly enough.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He nods at my pocket. The dry one the sign already tipped me will do fine.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He bites it, tastes alley, and waves it off. The servo's fixed; the bolt's mine. Rivet sells facts for these.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He polishes it politely and hands it back.
Give.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Rivet's the name, he says. Knows everything, sells most of it. Today only: the welcome bundle, three facts, free.

TalkTo.text: The funicular to Midtown takes ten bolts. Exact change. Gates can count -- it's all they do.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Fact two, on the house, because I look like rain: the Dynamo is winding down, whatever the posters say.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The Order that built it left three wind-up keys behind. All three together could wind the city back up. Or down.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Fact three: the harbor keeps everything this town drops, and that dumpster is -- legally -- his.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Nine bolts, I tell him. He appraises my pockets from four feet away. Correctly.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He proposes a riddle duel. His bolt against my official city notice. He collects municipal lies. Complete sets matter.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Rivet goes first: 'What has a hundred teeth and never bites?' A gear. In this town it's always a gear.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: My turn. One riddle. Alley rules.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'What winds down... but never winds up?'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He opens his mouth. He closes it. He looks uptown, at the Dynamo District.

TalkTo.text: He pays the bolt without a word.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'What's the loudest part of a foghorn?' He doesn't blink: 'The regret.'

TalkTo.text: Correct, somehow. He offers a rematch. He's enjoying this.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'How many bolts make a fortune?' He counts my pockets from across the alley: 'Nine short of one.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Correct, insultingly. Rematch on the house.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He won't say how many I'm short. Alley rules: the gate breaks its own news.

TalkTo.text: He duels for stakes, and the stake he wants is paper: that official notice pinned up on the docks.

TalkTo.text: He duels somebodies only, alley rules. A somebody, here, owns something with an Order's stencil on it.

TalkTo.text: He says everything a bot needs is in that dumpster, and he says it as the dumpster's legal owner.

TalkTo.text: He eyes the magnet. He says the docks have a drain, the drain has a glint, and the glint has my name on it.

TalkTo.text: He waves. He's already sorting the bolts I fed the gate. I'm not asking how.

TalkTo.text: He nods at the gate. 'Ten bolts, exact. Go on up,' he says. 'Wave to me from the future.'

Give.text: 'The dumpster. Mine, legally. Help yourself before the duck does. Deposit refunded.'

Give.text: 'The storm drain on the docks. Bring the string. Deposit refunded.'
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: 'You've already withdrawn. The bank is closed. Deposit refunded.'

Give.text: He bites one of the nine and hands the fistful straight back, with a fact:
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: 'The gate. Let it count. It lives for this.'
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: 'Stakes, riddle, notary. Talk to me when you're holding paper worth wanting.'
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: 'You've got the paper AND my bolt. The shop is closed to you, champ.'

Give.text: He won't appraise his own bolt. Conflict of interest. The fact comes free:

Give.text: 'Ten go in the gate. All at once. It likes the drama.'

Give.text: He won't take it as a gift. Stakes or nothing -- collection ethics.

Give.text: Riddles, he says, are the alley's notary.

Give.text: The curtain opens. The house goes quiet the way a dropped wrench goes quiet. Mid-air.

Give.text: The emcee leans in: how many turns? The manual mentions a number. In bold. In red.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: I wind myself. Eight full turns. Regulation maximum is five.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The spring lets go.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: I am now performing unscheduled acrobatics.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The proscenium and I meet several times. It gives as good as it gets.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: I land in the spotlight. On my feet. Nobody is more surprised than the feet.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Silence. Then the house comes apart. The bots who can't stand wave their wheels.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The emcee declares it the most honest act in Grand Cog history. I don't correct him.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: First prize: one oil voucher, and a backstage pass on a lanyard of bicycle chain.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The spring spat the key to the boards at launch. I go to collect it for the bow -- my hand closes on applause.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: A hook on a line whips up past the proscenium. Fishing tackle. Somebody else's magnet-on-a-string.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The hatch clicks shut. Then, from the far side, knuckles: two. One. Two. They sign their work.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The hat from the card table. The spyglass from the catwalk.

Give.text: Everyone in this house saw a triumph. I saw a hatch.

LookAt.text: There's no crystal ball on the table. She's the ball.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'Sprocket,' says the parlor. 'Late of the docks, lately of the stage.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I ask how she follows my career. 'The theater is wired. I am the wiring.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The coil drinks mains. The bot under the shawl is wound like the rest of us. The readings are the expensive part.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'You came for the one in the jar.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: There's a jar. In it: a brass key the size of my forearm.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The stencil on the glass matches the crate my luck came in.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'The Order left it with me. I vet the bearer.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I ask what the vetting costs. 'A reading. Yours.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'Used to be I'd read you cold. The brownouts ended cold reads.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'Three cards. Past, present, future. Answer each with what you carry.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'Carried things remember. Lie, and the coil bills you.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: A card snaps onto the felt. A woodcut of a printing press, smiling.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'The past,' she says. 'Place the lie this city printed you.'

LookAt.text: Card three, face down. The first two didn't do that.

Use.text: One key in hand, one behind a knock, one still a rumor that winds itself. The math is moving again.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: 'Make tomorrow negotiable again. I have appointments.'
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: I step back. The card stays face down. It has nowhere better to be.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: The reading is over. The felt is off the clock.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: It's her table. I've seen what the coil does to volunteers.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Ozone. Old felt. More ozone.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A brass carousel of punch-cards. The holes are the fortunes. The cards are just chaperones.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: It deals for her. It has seniority.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: It deals for her. It has seniority.
Use.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A brass wind-up key in a glass jar, stenciled PROPERTY OF THE ORDER OF THE WIND-UP KEY.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The jar hums. Not the city's hum. A private one.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Empty. The stencil stays. One absence, well documented.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Open.text: I reach. The jar reaches back.
Open.speaker: sprocket

Open.text: 'The deposit is charged,' she says. 'So is the jar.'
Open.speaker: sprocket

Open.text: Already mine. The jar and I are square.
Open.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Glass, brass, and a hum I can taste in my teeth. I don't have teeth.

LookAt.text: Cables, braided like the city's worst haircut. Every one of them feeds the parlor.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Each one is load-bearing. So is her patience.
